articles.summary: While some are worried about the use of AI in cyberattacks, Orca

articles.title: Security is using it to build better defenses. This blog post
articles.summary: shows how AI-powered security enables companies to:

articles.title: → Read the full article on the Orca blog
articles.summary: → See how you can simplify investigations and accelerate remediation

articles.title: ANTHROPIC LAUNCHES CLAUDE 3.5 SONNET
articles.summary: Claude 3.5 Sonnet passes Opus in performance at 1/5 the cost. It is also the best vision model available now. This highlights the improved capabilities of the frontier models.

articles.title: APPLE RESEARCHERS ADD 20 MORE OPEN-SOURCE MODELS TO IMPROVE TEXT AND IMAGE AI
articles.summary: Apple has contributed 20 Core Machine Learning models to the Hugging Face open-source AI repository, enhancing its existing range of public models with advances in image classification and depth segmentation. These contributions follow Apple's release of the Ferret large language model and the four OpenELMs to Hugging Face earlier in the year. The move demonstrates Apple's increasing engagement with the AI research community and its commitment to advancing AI capabilities.

articles.title: FACTORY'S SERIES A ANNOUNCEMENT
articles.summary: Factory has secured a $15M Series A funding, led by Sequoia Capital, to expand its team and enhance its suite of AI-powered software development tools known as Droids. Its products have achieved a new state-of-the-art on the SWE-bench AI coding benchmark and are driving rapid growth in its customer base. Factory aims to further automate software engineering, reducing tedious tasks and improving development cycles.

articles.title: OPTIMIZING INFERENCE AT CHARACTER AI
articles.summary: Character AI serves 20,000 queries per second, which is 20% of Google's search volume. It has a number of innovations to run this efficiently.

articles.title: REMOTE SENSING CHANGE DETECTION
articles.summary: ChangeViT is a framework that utilizes vision transformers (ViTs) for detecting large-scale environmental changes in remote sensing images.

articles.title: PRUNING WITH LAYERMERGE
articles.summary: LayerMerge is a new method that improves neural network efficiency by jointly pruning convolution layers and activation functions.

articles.title: CAN WE TRUST VISION-LANGUAGE MODELS?
articles.summary: Vision-enabled language models (VLMs) like GPT-4o and Gemini power autonomous agents capable of tasks such as making purchases or editing code. This work highlights the vulnerability of these agents to malicious attacks.

articles.title: EVALUATING WEB AGENTS IN REAL-TIME
articles.summary: WebCanvas is a new framework for evaluating autonomous web agents in dynamic, live web environments.

articles.title: 94% ACCURACY ON CIFAR-10 IN 3.29 SECONDS
articles.summary: CIFAR-10 is an image classification benchmark. This code provides a training configuration that achieves good performance in an amazingly short time.

articles.title: TIME SERIES FORECASTING WITH TIMESIEVE
articles.summary: TimeSieve is a new model designed to tackle common challenges in time series forecasting.

articles.title: COST OF SELF-HOSTING LLAMA-3 8B-INSTRUCT
articles.summary: Self-hosting an LLM like Llama-3 8B-Instruct can be significantly more expensive than using ChatGPT, costing around $17 per million tokens compared to ChatGPT's $1 per million tokens. The cost of self-hosted hardware can be reduced to less than $0.01 per million tokens, though it would take approximately 5.5 years to break even on the initial investment.

articles.title: KHROMA (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Khroma uses AI to learn which colors you like and creates limitless palettes for you to discover, search, and save.

articles.title: NEW BENCHMARK FOR DEPTH NORMAL ESTIMATION
articles.summary: A new benchmark evaluates state-of-the-art depth and surface normal estimation models.

articles.title: NVIDIA RELEASES MAMBA 2 (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: The hybrid simple Mamba model recently highlighted in an Nvidia paper has been released.
